content: R0 informs public health intervention thresholds. To achieve herd immunity through vaccination, the proportion needed is calculated as (1 - 1/R0). For measles (R0=15), approximately 93% vaccination coverage is required. Interventions reduce effective reproduction number (Rt): isolation reduces infectious period, masks reduce transmission probability, social distancing reduces contact rate. During outbreaks, Rt monitoring guides intervention intensity. When Rt drops below 1, case numbers decline exponentially. Real-time Rt estimation uses case data with generation time distributions.
source: WHO outbreak response guidelines, CDC MMWR
